status: PARTIAL — TRIAD-INT4 v1 bundle measured at N=10 on Z Flip7; runbook still owns v2 calibration + baselines + reference

device_runs.bundle_origin: v1 TRIAD-INT4 calibration, q4f16_1 export, session-3 install

thermal_finding.interpretation: Sustained-generation decode is 5-6 tok/s slower than cold-cache short-generation decode on Xclipse 950 — thermal throttling kicks in over the course of a 500-600-token generation. The session-3 N=3 value of 35.56 tok/s reflected only the short-generation regime; the N=10 value with mixed completion lengths is 31.40 ± 4.80 tok/s.

supersedes_published_claim.n10_replacement: 31.40 ± 4.80 tok/s (N=6 successful out of 10) covering both short and long completion regimes
supersedes_published_claim.honest_published_claim: TRIAD-INT4 sustained decode on Xclipse 950: 31.4 ± 4.8 tok/s; short-completion decode ~35 tok/s; long-completion decode ~29 tok/s.

reference_baseline_q4f16_1.reason: The reference q4f16_1 community-baseline bundle is not currently installed on the device. Only the TRIAD-INT4 bundle exists in /data/data/ai.mlc.mlcchat/files/. To run the paired-t comparison the runbook must (1) download the canonical TinyLlama-1.1B q4f16_1 bundle from MLC's HF repo, (2) adb-push it as a second model row, (3) re-run the bench with BENCH_BASELINE_JSON pointing at the ref JSON to get the paired-t output.

v2_calibration.reason: v2 calibration on TinyLlama-1.1B requires either a 4090 host (per the v2 plan budget, ≤30 min) or a long M1 run with safe_cholesky_inverse (Phase A6 unblocks but adds CPU/fp64 overhead). On the M1 dev host the v2 pipeline runs end-to-end on synthetic LlamaConfig fixtures (Phase H integration test), but no real model has been calibrated.

ppl_eval_matrix.reason: WT2 PPL on Llama-3.2-1B / TinyLlama-1.1B / SmolLM-360M / SmolLM-135M for v2 + the 6 baseline runners requires CUDA. The runners under experiments/baselines/ currently emit skipped:no_cuda on M1.

group_size_sweep_on_device.reason: Three pre-calibrated bundles (G ∈ {32, 64, 128}) need to be staged on the phone before ADR-015's gate can resolve from 'Provisional' to 'Accepted: G=...'.
